cascade.designation: 500 kVA KPLC TX 415V TPN LV terminals

selectivity_implications.recommendation: MCCB 100A Icu 25 kA — adequate against 9.80 kA at MSP-100 intake (2.6× margin). Verify Ics ≥ 75% Icu at full rating; KS 1700:2018 §434.5 (routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §434.5.1 for short-circuit breaking capacity).

MCCB 63A Icu 10 kA — adequate against 7.24 kA at GH-DB incoming (1.38× margin — marginal). Consider upgrading to Icu 15 kA for prudent design margin; cascade discrimination with upstream MSP MCCB verified by 60m × 95mm² submain impedance step (25.87 → 34.77 mΩ).

assumptions: KPLC declared Ze=0.80 Ω and PSCC=9.8 kA at MSP-100 intake (TN-S) treated as binding boundary per KS 1700:2018 §313.1 (routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §313.1 for the maximum prospective fault-current ascertainment rule); HV-side modelling supplied for cascade audit only.

KS 1700:2018 §312 routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §313.1 for short-circuit interrupting verification (direct citation form per Kenya jurisdictional policy; KS 1700:2018 Annex E adopts the BS 7671 short-circuit chain verbatim).
Transformer X/R = 5 assumed for 500 kVA KPLC pole-mount distribution unit per IEC 60909-1:2008 Table A.1.
Cable impedance per IEC 60364-5-52:2009 Annex E: 70mm² Cu XLPE @ 90°C R=0.337 mΩ/m, X=0.0875 mΩ/m; 95mm² Cu XLPE @ 90°C R=0.208 mΩ/m, X=0.0829 mΩ/m.

Peak factor κ computed per IEC 60909-0:2016 Eq. 29: κ = 1.02 + 0.98·exp(-3/(X/R)) with κ regenerated at each node from the local X/R. κ ≈ 1.56 at TX-1 (X/R≈4.98), ≈ 1.50 at MSP-100 (X/R≈4.18), ≈ 1.49 at MSP busbar (X/R≈4.13) and ≈ 1.17 at GH-DB (X/R≈1.62) reflecting the resistive contribution of the 60m submain.

Motor contribution disregarded: total connected motor load = 37 kW → ΣI_rM ≈ 71 A at 415V (η=0.85, cosφ=0.85); IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.8.1 threshold = 1% × 9800 A = 98 A; ΣI_rM < threshold → motor contribution explicitly excluded from the cascade.
Selectivity verification per KS 1700:2018 §434.5 + §536.4 (routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §434.5.1 for breaking capacity and §536.4 for OCPD discrimination).
tool_call_pending: true — calc.iec60909_cascade deterministic execution pending per shared runtime contract.

rationale.chat_summary: KE Nairobi industrial workshop on KPLC TN-S 415V. 5-node cascade: 11kV → 500 kVA TX → MSP-100 (100A) → busbar → 60m × 95mm² submain → GH-DB. KPLC-declared 9.8 kA at MSP-100 binds the cascade per KS 1700:2018 §313.1. Ik"max range 7.24–13.00 kA; HV-1 13.00 kA. Motor load 37 kW: ΣI_rM=71 A < §3.8.1 threshold 98 A — motor contribution EXCLUDED. MCCB 25 kA + 10 kA verified.

sections.title: Source Specification
sections.summary: Single-source utility supply: 500 kVA KPLC pole-mount transformer fed from 11 kV radial primary. No standby generator, no UPS. Treated as far-from-generator per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.4.

decisions.label: Source modelling — far-from-generator
decisions.summary: Utility transformer with no rotating generation on bus during fault; constant Thévenin source.

sections.title: Cascade Topology
sections.summary: 5-node engineer-declared cascade mirrors the SLD topology (ke-nairobi-industrial-msb-gh): HV-1 → TX-1 → MSP-100 → MSP-100.BUS → GH-DB. Linear two-board cascade; no parallel branches.

decisions.label: Engineer-declared topology
decisions.summary: Author-supplied cascade matching the architectural drawing rather than inferred from a board-graph.

sections.title: HV-side Assumptions
sections.summary: 11 kV KPLC radial supply, declared PSCC 13 kA at primary, X/R = 5 (typical urban distribution per IEC 60909-1:2008 Table A.1). Applying HV voltage factor c_max = 1.10 per IEC 60909-0:2016 Table 1: ZQ_HV = (1.10 × 11000) / (√3 × 13000) ≈ 0.5374 Ω. Referred to LV via turns ratio (415/11000)² ≈ 1.42 × 10⁻³, the HV contribution at LV terminals is ≈0.76 µΩ — negligible against transformer impedance but retained in the cascade audit.

decisions.label: c_max = 1.10 HV
decisions.summary: Voltage factor selected for Un = 11 kV per IEC 60909-0:2016 Table 1.

sections.title: Transformer + Source Impedances
sections.summary: Z_TX = 0.0475 × 415² / 500 000 = 16.36 mΩ at LV (isolated, infinite-source assumption); with X/R = 5: R_TX = 3.21 mΩ, X_TX = 16.04 mΩ. An isolated calculation would yield Ik" = 1.05 × 415 / (√3 × 16.36 mΩ) ≈ 15.4 kA — but the KPLC-declared 9.8 kA at MSP-100 binds the cascade per KS 1700:2018 §313.1 (routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §313.1) and implies higher upstream impedance than the nameplate value alone, typical of long rural 11 kV feeders into pole-mount distribution units. TX-1 is back-calculated as ≈10.22 kA accounting for the 3m × 70mm² Cu XLPE service cable to MSP-100.

decisions.label: KPLC declaration is binding
decisions.summary: KS 1700:2018 §313.1 (routing to BS 7671:2018+A2 §313.1) requires using utility-declared values for design. The isolated TX calculation would yield ≈15.4 kA at LV terminals; the binding 9.8 kA at MSP-100 reflects the actual upstream network including HV feeder impedance, transformer impedance, and the 3m service cable.
decisions.rule: KS 1700:2018 §313.1 — Maximum prospective fault current to be ascertained (Annex E adopts BS 7671:2018+A2 §313.1 verbatim)

sections.title: Motor Contribution Assessment
sections.summary: Connected motor inventory: 11 kW lathe + 4 kW pillar drill + 22 kW air compressor = 37 kW total. Per IEC 60909-0:2016 §3.8.1 threshold check, ΣI_rM at 415V (η=0.85, cosφ=0.85) ≈ 71 A < 1% × 9800 A = 98 A. Threshold NOT met → motor contribution EXCLUDED from the cascade. The §3.8.1 rule is followed strictly; this is the engineering-honest call even though the 22 kW compressor sits close to the MSP-100 busbar.

decisions.label: Motor contribution excluded per §3.8.1
decisions.summary: ΣI_rM = 71 A is below the 1% threshold (98 A). No asynchronous-motor sub-transient contribution added to sources[]. If workshop expands or adds large mechanical plant, re-run with updated motor inventory.

sections.title: Per-node Ifault Computation
sections.summary: Ik"max applied with c=1.05 per Table 1 (LV). Ik"max range across cascade: HV-1 13.00 kA → TX-1 10.22 → MSP-100 9.80 → MSP-100.BUS 9.78 → GH-DB 7.24 kA. Submain cable impedance per IEC 60364-5-52:2009 Annex E (95mm² Cu XLPE: R=0.208 mΩ/m, X=0.0829 mΩ/m; over 60m: R=12.48 mΩ, X=4.97 mΩ). Peak factor κ from IEC 60909-0:2016 Eq. 29.

decisions.label: c_max = 1.05 LV; c_min = 0.95 LV
decisions.summary: Per IEC 60909-0:2016 Table 1 for low-voltage networks ≤ 1 kV.

decisions.label: tool_call_pending across cascade
decisions.summary: Per-node Ik"max/Ik"min/ipk values emitted as engineer estimates; deterministic recompute requires calc.iec60909_cascade tool execution.

sections.title: Selectivity Implications
sections.summary: MSP-100 MCCB 100A Icu 25 kA verified against 9.80 kA cascade (2.6× margin). GH-DB MCCB 63A Icu 10 kA verified against 7.24 kA (1.38× margin — marginal; recommend Icu 15 kA upgrade for design prudence). All comply with KS 1700:2018 §434.5 (routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §434.5.1). Cascade discrimination supported by the 60m submain impedance step (25.87 → 34.77 mΩ) per KS 1700:2018 §536.4.

decisions.label: Breaking-capacity verification
decisions.summary: Device Icu ratings exceed applied prospective fault at each node; GH-DB headroom is marginal at 1.38× and flagged for upgrade.
decisions.rule: KS 1700:2018 §434.5 — Devices for fault current protection (routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §434.5.1)

sections.title: Compliance + Assumptions
sections.summary: Cascade compliant with KS 1700:2018 + IEC 60909-0:2016. Explicit assumptions: KPLC declaration binding; transformer X/R from IEC 60909-1:2008 Table A.1; cable Z from IEC 60364-5-52:2009 Annex E; motor contribution disregarded per §3.8.1 strict threshold; KS 1700:2018 Annex E routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §313.1 / §434.5.1 / §536.4 for the short-circuit chain; calc.iec60909_cascade tool execution pending.

decisions.label: KS 1700:2018 §443 SPD recommendation
decisions.summary: Type 1/2 SPD recommended at MSP-100 origin for atmospheric-origin transient protection — Nairobi sits on the Rift escarpment with elevated keraunic level (40–80 thunder-days/year); KS 1700:2018 §443 routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §443.4 verbatim.
decisions.rule: KS 1700:2018 §443.4 — Protection against transient overvoltages of atmospheric origin (routes to BS 7671:2018+A2 §443.4)
